customManagers.description: One update surface for every {tag, commit} pin to the policy repo (ADR-0002 §6.2): the fleet's nested {version, tag, commit} array (policy-versions.yaml) is the only pin surface in this design -- consumer apps opt in via a mycompany.com/policy-version label only, they carry no git ref of their own to bump (see issue 11's comments for why). Renovate's native flux manager can't maintain a {tag, commit} pair -- it tracks one or the other, exclusively -- so this customManager (git-refs datasource) does. Matches `tag:`/`commit:` adjacent lines, not `version:` -- `version` only diverges from `tag` for a CI-only-fix patch release (rare), and a human reviewing the PR (never automerged) adjusts it by hand in that case.

packageRules.description: No live semver ranges anywhere (ADR-0002): every match from the customManager above is pinned exact, range strategy is never in play, but pin this down explicitly as a guardrail.
